section_3_learning_objectives.note: 6 Bloom-level objectives covering all concepts introduced.

section_4_introduction.note: 3 paragraphs; opens with calculator analogy before introducing terms.

section_5_core_concepts.note: Five numbered subsections (5.1.1 through 5.2.5) with full prose, annotated code examples, and definitions for every concept introduced.

section_10_key_takeaways.note: 5 substantive bullets; each is a standalone assertion the learner should be able to state independently.

section_5.note: All return-keyword rules carry [1]; parameter/argument definitions carry [2] per bullet; implicit-None mechanic carries [1][3]; vending-machine analogy carries [2]; 'one definition many inputs' carries [2][3]; print-vs-return conclusion carries [1][2].

section_6.note: Steps 2, 3, 4, 5, 7 each carry [1] or [2]. Step 6 (read the output) and the spec-comment line are procedural/display steps with no factual claim to cite — acceptable omissions.

section_7.note: Pattern 1 carries [1][3]; Pattern 2 carries [1][2]; Pattern 3 carries [1]; None-flag explanation carries [3]. Introductory framing sentence ('bread and butter') carries [1]. At threshold.

cycle_1_failures_resolved: sum() replaced with explicit loop — §7 Pattern 1 now uses the for-loop accumulator shown in §6; no sum() appears anywhere in the file.
§7 Pattern 2 file-I/O block (with open, for line in f, .strip(), .append(), int()) is removed; replaced with format_report(student_name, score) which stays within prior concepts.
marks[0] initialisation removed; §7 Pattern 1 uses the None-flag pattern (highest = None) instead.
§6 Implementation citation density improved from 14% to 75%.
§5 Core Concepts citation density improved from 38% to 74%.

cleared_hard_failures_from_cycle_1.resolution: Removed. Replaced with explicit for-loop accumulator throughout §7 Pattern 1 and §8. No occurrence of sum() in corpus.

cleared_hard_failures_from_cycle_1.term: file I/O (with open / for line in f / .strip() / .append() / int())
cleared_hard_failures_from_cycle_1.resolution: Removed. §7 Pattern 2 now uses format_report(student_name, score) — operates only on strings and the classify_mark return value, both within prior concepts.
Removed. find_highest and find_lowest now initialise with highest = None / lowest = None and use the is-None check on first iteration.

soft_findings.assessment: str() is used as a built-in type-conversion call without prior introduction in the listed concepts for topics 11.3–12.1. It appears in code examples only (not in prose definitions), in a string-concatenation context where its purpose is inferable from context. Per the task-level scope note, str() for string concatenation is a 'common beginner pattern' and the critic should 'use judgment.' The usage is contextually clear; the term is not formally defined here (so it is not taught as a forward concept). Escalated to soft finding for HITL #3 awareness.
soft_findings.recommendation: Add a one-line gloss when str() first appears in §7: 'str(score) converts the number to text so it can be joined to the string — Python requires all parts of a joined string to be the same type.'

cleared_soft_from_cycle_1.resolution: Phrase 'No global variables' retained in §7 Pattern 3 commentary but now presented as a consequence visible from the code (reader can observe there are no global variables), not as a defined concept. Low-severity; acceptable.
Still present in §7 Pattern 3: 'clean separation of concerns [1].' Used as a descriptive label (one sentence, carries [1]) rather than a taught concept. Low-severity; acceptable.

Retained in §8 Best Practices common-mistake block as error output only ('TypeError: unsupported operand type(s) for +: NoneType and int'). Not taught; shown as consequence. Soft finding retained from cycle 1 but does not escalate.

remaining_noted_items.note: Used in §6 Step 3 with an inline plain-English gloss: 'len(marks) gives the number of items in the list — a built-in that tells you the length.' This constitutes an adequate first-use explanation for a self-explanatory built-in. Acceptable per scope note.
Named in one sentence in §5.1.3 with explicit 'covered in later topics' pointer. Mention-and-defer form is compliant — not bolded, not defined.
classify_mark is defined in §5.2.2 (lines 140–150) and Pattern 3. Not a forward reference.
None introduced by this corpus in §5.2.4. The 'highest is None' pattern in §7 Pattern 1 post-dates that introduction. Not a forward reference.

length_sanity.counting_method: all prose, code comments, and code block text; excluding YAML frontmatter lines 1–7
length_sanity.note: Estimated count ~2919 is 2.7% below the 3000 floor — well within the 30% tolerance band (failure requires >30% outside range, i.e. <2100). PASS. Soft advisory: corpus is lean for standard depth. The removal of the file-I/O block (scope creep from cycle 1) accounts for the shortfall. If future revision occurs, expanding §7 commentary or adding a fourth real-world pattern would reach 3000+. No action required this cycle.

cycle_1_failures_resolved: §7 Pattern 2 file-I/O block removed. Replaced with format_report(student_name, score) — a string-building function using only parameters, return values, and classify_mark (all within this topic's scope).
sum() removed from §7 Pattern 1 and §8; explicit loop used throughout.
marks[0] removed; None-flag initialisation used instead.
remaining_scope_notes: 'separation of concerns' in §7 Pattern 3 is used as a descriptive phrase (one sentence, cited [1]) rather than a taught concept — compliant.
TypeError in §8 appears as code output only — not taught. Acceptable.

terms: str() — used in §7 Pattern 2 and §9 without plain-language definition (also flagged in sequence_drift.soft_findings)

jargon_dumps.note: Parameter and argument introduced together in §5.1.1 but via separate bullets, plain-language vending-machine analogy, and the 'where you are standing' contrast frame — compliant with the one-new-idea-at-a-time mandate.

concrete_examples_before_abstraction.note: §4 leads with calculator / light-switch analogy. §5.1.1 uses vending machine before formal definitions. §5.2.1 gives working add(a, b) code before stating the return-mechanism rule.

cycle_1_soft_findings_resolved: 'separation of concerns' — still present as a descriptive phrase but no longer counts as an undefined term requiring resolution.
'TypeError' — still present as error output; not a teaching term. Soft finding noted but below escalation threshold.

aggregate_soft_findings.action: Surface at HITL #3 for author awareness. Recommendation: add one-line inline gloss on first use in §7.

aggregate_soft_findings.detail: Word count estimated ~2919, marginally below the 3000 standard-profile floor (2.7% below). Within 30% tolerance band — not a hard fail. Shortfall attributable to removal of cycle-1 scope-creep material (file-I/O block). Informational only.

verdict: PASS — cycle 2. All six hard checks clear. Four hard failures from cycle 1 are resolved: sum() replaced with explicit loop throughout, file-I/O block (with open / .strip() / .append() / int()) removed from §7 Pattern 2 and replaced with a within-scope format_report function, marks[0] list-indexing removed and replaced with the None-flag pattern, and citation density lifted from 37% to 71% overall (§6 from 14% to 75%, §5 from 38% to 74%). Two soft findings remain: str() is used without definition in §7 Pattern 2 and §9 (soft — one undefined term, below the two-term soft-fail threshold; recommend inline gloss on first use); word count ~2919 is 2.7% below the 3000 standard floor but well within the 30% tolerance band (soft advisory only). Corpus is cleared to ground reading, quiz, and diagram artifacts.
